response.summary: The full health plan screen is structurally present, but key user-facing semantics do not meet the slice contract: the screen does not clearly communicate that it is scoped to the active profile, and the totals summary introduces ambiguous dashboard-driven status language instead of staying in the user-facing Due/Planned/Done model.

findings.observed: The visible heading stack ('Full health plan' / 'Your complete preventive care plan') never makes clear that this is the plan for the active profile. For a per-profile plan view in a product that supports multiple profiles, the screen reads as a generic plan screen rather than a clearly profile-scoped one.

The totals helper says 'Open dashboard items: {count}. Completed items: {count}.' This introduces an extra 'Open dashboard items' concept tied to another surface instead of using the slice’s unified user-facing status model of Due, Planned, or Done. The wording is unclear to users and drifts into system/surface mechanics rather than meaningful plan status context.

The lead copy ('Review every recommended care step in one place, then open any item for details and completion.') mainly describes what the screen lets the user do. It does not clearly explain the user value of this full-plan view beyond the dashboard, such as helping the user check everything for this profile in one place.
